What is a Model?
H
ab
T
A model is an idealized representation of a system.
1380664
Example:
We model the fall of an object on Earth as subject to a constant
acceleration of 9.81 m/s2 due to gravity.
While this describes the behavior of our system, it is merely
an approximation.
It doesn't account for the effects of air resistance, local
variations in gravity, etc.
But in practice, it's accurate enough to be useful!
